---
title: "Machine Learning Foundations"
page-layout: full
---

## Course Overview

INFO 521 is a 7.5-week graduate course covering the foundations of machine learning along
one arc — **loss → likelihood → posterior → approximation → discovery** — from classical
linear regression through Bayesian inference to clustering and PCA, all on a single
clinical dataset (NHANES). The course emphasizes both theoretical understanding and
practical application, combining interactive peer-engagement loops with projects that
integrate the full learning arc. *(Neural networks continue in the sequel, INFO 557.)*

:::{.grid}

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Slides

Fourteen reveal.js decks across seven modules — linear models, generalization, MLE,
Bayesian inference, approximate inference, SVM & clustering, and PCA — with dark/light
toggle and a PDF alongside every deck.

[Browse Slides →](slides.qmd)
:::

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Activities

Six weekly peer-engagement loops: interactive tool + built-in mastery check → reflection → peer review of every teammate → respond & revise. Each tool is a self-contained visualization with a retakeable quiz.

[Explore Activities →](activities.qmd)
:::

:::{.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Assessment

Specifications grading — every assignment is pass/not-yet with revise-and-resubmit built in, never partial credit. Components combine by published weights: Projects 55% · Homework 30% · Checkpoints 9% · Peer loops 5% · Prereq quiz 1%.

[See Grading Scheme →](assessment.qmd)
:::

:::

## Schedule

The course follows a **module-by-module schedule** — one module per week, each pairing
slides, readings, and a hands-on activity loop:

[Module Schedule →](schedule.qmd)

---

## Key Resources

- **[Syllabus](syllabus.qmd)**: the full course policies — also as a [PDF](syllabus.pdf)
- **[Homeworks](homeworks.qmd)**: eight specs-graded deliverables, distributed via GitHub Classroom (invite link posted on D2L at term start)
- **Projects**: one clinical dataset carries the semester arc across two projects — *One Problem, Three Lenses* (due Wk 4) and *From Inference to Discovery* (due Wk 7)
- **Checkpoints**: five short, retakeable derivation checks (Weeks 2–6) that gate the project milestones
